name: Eddie Locke
abstract: Eddie Locke (August 2, 1930 – September 7, 2009) was an American jazz drummer. Eddie Locke was a part of the fertile and vibrant Detroit jazz scene during the 1940s and 1950s, which brought forth many great musicians including the Jones brothers (Hank, Thad, and Elvin), Kenny Burrell, Lucky Thompson, Tommy Flanagan, Barry Harris, and so …

interviews.title: Eddie Locke interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, NY, January 13, 2001

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Dick Hyman and Friends

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: Oliver Jackson was a brilliant young drummer whom I had met at the Metropole. That had been a team with Eddie Locke, who was another fine young drummer from Detroit, who had become part of Jo Jones’s entourage.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: You know two little kids that stayed with me, Oliver Jackson and Eddie Locke. I used to tell them, 'You're going to play with Earl Hines...'

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Locke…I can’t think of his name right now, the other bop… It was another drummer that was Eddie Locke’s best friend.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Cascadu
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Eddie Locke the drummer talked about carrying Joe Jones’ drums or something. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, man.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cascadu interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Locke and his group, and Jerry Sokolov and his group, and the Trio, it’s gonna be quite a long day of events.
about_quotes.doc_title: Percy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Catherine Russell
about_quotes.evidence: I did my first swing dance with Earl May with the great bass player Earl May. And Eddie Locke was on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Catherine Russell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 26, 2018
